channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Protesters claim to be in chains at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment
item.description: A protester has told 891 ABC Adelaide she has chained herself to a gate on War Memorial Drive and that four others have chained themselves to machinery inside the North Adelaide Golf Course.

media:group.media:description: The ABC has been told as well as protesters standing outside the golf club, more have entered the site.

item.title: Why Millennials and gen Z feel ripped off by this budget
item.description: The prime minister said this budget was about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the Millennials are hearing a different narrative.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Commentators online say the sweeping tax changes in the federal budget advantage Boomers.

item.title: Melanie Lynskey joins the fight for justice after NZ's Pike River Mine disaster
item.description: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.
item.dc:creator: Stephen A Russell

media:group.media:description: Twenty-nine men died in 2010 after a series of explosions in the Pike River Mine. The youngest had just turned 17.

item.title: Surfers conquer 'terrifying' tidal rapid in remote WA
item.description: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Talbot Bay tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jackson

media:group.media:description: Big wave surfer Anthony Walsh was the first to ride the tidal rapid.

item.title: WA Premier Roger Cook treads fine line in 'standing up to Canberra'
item.description: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: Roger Cook says his government will stand up to Canberra when it needs to.

item.title: Man charged with murder over mother's death at inner north Canberra home
item.description: The man has been held in police custody since being arrested at the scene and is due in court today.

media:group.media:description: A large area around the home on Dryandra Street was cordoned off yesterday as forensic officers were on the scene.

item.title: Live: 'Productive' first day of Israel and Lebanon talks as Trump discusses Iran with Xi
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Georgie Hewson

media:group.media:description: Emergency responders gather around a burning vehicle at the scene of an Israeli airstrike in the coastal town of Jiyeh, south of Beirut, Lebanon, Wednesday, May 13.

item.title: Min Woo Lee leads PGA Championship
item.description: Australian Min Woo Lee enjoys a share of the first-round clubhouse lead, but Masters champ Rory McIlroy has endured a rough start to the PGA Championship.

media:group.media:description: Min Woo Lee has a share of the clubhouse lead.

item.title: Playful seals looking for a feed increasingly snared by fishing lines
item.description: Data from the wildlife rescue group ORRCA shows an increase in the number of fur seal entanglements as they seek out fishing lines in attempts to find an easy meal.
item.dc:creator: Toby Hemmings and Alasdair McDonald

media:group.media:description: Fur seal with rope around neck.

item.title: Live: ASX set to open higher as Wall Street rallies with new records
item.description: The ASX is set to open higher in morning trading after rallies on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in December 2022.

item.title: Ten still in hospital after bus rollover in north Queensland
item.description: Queensland police have described a "confronting scene" at a bus crash on a remote stretch of the Bruce Highway on Thursday afternoon.

media:group.media:description: The bus rolled off the roadway on the Bruce Highway.

item.title: Indigenous housing collapse puts families at risk of homelessness
item.description: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.
item.dc:creator: Tim Little and Zara Margolis

media:group.media:description: Kerry Major faces possible eviction after the corporation she rents from racked up unpaid rates.

item.title: Why the Italian Open tennis is causing chaos in Italy's top football league
item.description: The race for the Champions League spots has been overshadowed as Serie A serves up fixture chaos relating to the Italian Open tennis tournament in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Lazio want to move their match at the Stadio Olimpico against Roma so it does not clash with the Italian Open tennis.

item.title: Live: Taylor 'huffing and puffing on the dog whistle': Labor criticises budget reply
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler says the group Australia repatriated from the hantavirus cruise ship are in "good shape" ahead of their arrival in Perth today. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

media:group.media:description: Federal Health Minister, Mark Butler.

item.title: Shed weight, go slow and pump up your tyres to save on fuel
item.description: How to reduce your petrol bill by changing your driving technique and tinkering with your car.
item.dc:creator: Gavin McGrath

media:group.media:description: The federal government's National Fuel Security Plan website includes fuel-saving tips.

item.title: Delta Goodrem climbs onto her piano and into Eurovision 2026 grand final
item.description: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem performs at the Eurovision 2026 semifinal.

item.title: Northern Territory musicians escape to international festival in the UK
item.description: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.
item.dc:creator: Anzaya Karim

media:group.media:description: Sally Balfour says the festival is an opportunity for regional musicians to connect with the industry.

item.title: Why four lanes might not be better than two on this Canberra road
item.description: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore and Harry Frost

media:group.media:description: William Hovell Drive is one of three Canberra roads currently slated for duplication work.

item.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch
item.description: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope

media:group.media:description: Air guns towed by a survey vessel produce loud noises that can be used to detect oil and gas below the sea floor.

item.title: Giro crash is Aussies' gain as he enjoys 'Bradbury moment' in Napoli
item.description: Jensen Plowright never expected to be contesting for stage victories at this year's Giro d'Italia. But the withdrawal of Kaden Groves and yet another calamitous crash gave him his first chance on stage six.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Elmar Reinders appeared to hit the deck first in a relatively low-speed crash with 400 metres to go.

item.title: 'Incredible' colonial-era boat used for trade in Sydney Harbour uncovered
item.description: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Julia André

media:group.media:description: The remnants of a colonial-era boat were discovered during construction of the Barangaroo metro station in Sydney.

item.title: 'Different doctor every fortnight' is the reality for these residents
item.description: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: Strahan residents Jamie and Gaylene McGuinness have not had a regular GP since 2017.

item.title: Community decries government 'arrogance' over fracking bill
item.description: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.
item.dc:creator: Josh Brine, Liz Rymill, and Selina Green

media:group.media:description: Anti-fracking protesters at a country cabinet meeting in Mount Gambier last night.

item.title: 'Mega' gas-fired power stations could be the new normal, analyst says
item.description: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: Energy Australia's 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant has been proposed for Marulan in the Southern Highlands.

item.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected
item.description: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: It was recommended the Australian government fully remediate Wilkes Station by 2030.
